ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a ga hagɩrɩtieŋ tigiŋ me a ga gaarɩ ʋ nyinti, sie ʋ woliŋ bɔbɩ wa ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ gaarɩ ʋ tigiŋ nyinti mana.”
translated: The child who does not listen to the elder's words and does not respect the elder will not be respected when they grow up, even if they have a lot of money and many children.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: “Daa wa tʋntʋntɩŋ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, ‘Jakʋʋŋ, daa zaabʋrɩvɩɩna die fʋ bʋrɩ fʋ kʋakʋ ma? Ama sɩa huutiti dɩ nyɩŋ?’
translated: “The father of Tuntun and his family said, ‘Jakuuŋ, didn't your father teach you how to build a house? Or did you not listen to him?”

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Womi Piita dɩ huu dɩ, “Ŋmɩŋ datɩ n tɩbɩŋ dɩɩ yi maŋ ka bala wusie! N ka sɩba daa wa mi!” Womi kparaaŋ die dɩ kʋŋ,
translated: The woman said to Piita, "My husband is not at home, he has gone to the farm! I am going to the market!"

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ta dɩɩ yi nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie dɩ sɩba nɩ nagɩ nyɩnvɩɩna a yɩ nɩ ballɩ, die nɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma nan faasɩ yɩ vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩ wa nyɩnvɩɩna.
translated: They said that the child's mother was a twin and the child too was a twin, and that is why they went to the river to throw the child into the water so that the child would not bring bad luck to the mother.
